title: Disaster evidence photo and inventory log
summary: A safe documentation sequence for wide views, item details, serial information, dates, temporary repairs, expenses, and secure backup before cleanup changes the scene.
content:
A safe documentation sequence for wide views, item details, serial information, dates, temporary repairs, expenses, and secure backup before cleanup changes the scene.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Enter only after authorities and utility checks allow it; photograph the exterior and each room from safe positions before moving items.
Capture wide context, closer damage, model or serial information when reachable, water or soot lines, and a date-and-location note.
Number items in a written inventory and link each number to photographs, ownership evidence, estimated age, and immediate safety action.
Record temporary protective work and every expense, back up the evidence securely, and contact the insurer or aid agency through verified details before disposal when possible.
warnings: Documentation never justifies entering fire, collapse, sewage, electrical, chemical, or mould hazards.
Do not post detailed damage, addresses, documents, or valuables publicly.
common_mistakes: Starting Disaster evidence photo and inventory log without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Disaster evidence photo and inventory log.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Disaster evidence photo and inventory log when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Disaster evidence photo and inventory log when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Disaster evidence photo and inventory log.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Disaster evidence photo and inventory log safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Disaster evidence photo and inventory log when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Disaster evidence photo and inventory log.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Disaster evidence photo and inventory log should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Disaster evidence photo and inventory log whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Disaster evidence photo and inventory log in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Disaster evidence photo and inventory log after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Disaster evidence photo and inventory log cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Disaster evidence photo and inventory log are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Disaster evidence photo and inventory log to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Disaster evidence photo and inventory log.']
